old_claim.note: State Armories provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because military signal flags are visual coordination practices, and the corrected state_armories node is a later Qin bronze-weapon workshop and arsenal-logistics scope.

source_shape.source_locator: The Qin armory source anchors state_armories to the First Emperor mausoleum project beginning in 246 BCE and weapon-production logistics intensified after imperial unification in 221 BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a later Qin bronze-weapon production and logistics scope, not a prerequisite for earlier generic military signal flags.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A -500 visual-signaling node cannot depend on a -246 Qin armory scope; organized formations and roads preserve the relevant coordination context.

ontology_before: Military Signal Flags depended on State Armories, creating an unsourced and now chronologically invalid dependency after state_armories was source-checked to Qin evidence.
ontology_after: Military Signal Flags keeps formation and road context while state_armories remains available for later depot-record and arsenal-logistics paths.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: military_signal_flags no longer directly depends on state_armories.
would_reject_if: The military_signal_flags node were redated and rescoped specifically to Qin arsenal-issued military standards or flags.
A source showed early military signal flags required state-controlled weapon arsenals rather than command formations and movement infrastructure.
The state_armories node were split and a pre-500 BCE armory node with direct flag-equipment evidence were added.
short_reason: The corrected armory chronology is later than the signal-flags node and does not support a direct dependency.
